overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly focused and academically prestigious social media presence across specialized platforms. With a total following of 3,580 users, his content successfully targets researchers, data scientists, and computational social science enthusiasts. Across all seven analyzed posts, engagement remains remarkably high, driven by major professional milestones such as software releases, new research publications, grant announcements, and job opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, publications, and methodological approaches to studying coordinated link sharing and disinformation online.
Recruitment posts for postdoctoral researchers and PhD students in computational social science.
Announcements of new research funding, grants, and major software releases like CooRnet v2.0.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly active and responsive academic community by posting visual summaries, thread breakdowns, or direct links to newly published papers.
Utilize LinkedIn's strong absolute reach to expand hiring campaigns and professional announcements, perhaps incorporating media like team photos or infographics.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon (aoir.social) to establish a more consistent presence, targeting specific technical questions or tool documentations for CooRnet.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky represents the most highly engaged community with an average engagement rate of 6.14%. Academic opportunities and publication announcements perform exceptionally well here, gaining significant traction through reposts.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon yields a solid 4.55% average engagement rate, acting as a highly dedicated channel for specialized tools (like CooRnet) and deep methodological discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is the largest platform by follower count, generating the highest raw engagement counts (up to 107 actions on a single post). It serves as the primary platform for high-level professional announcements, such as new grants and hiring campaigns.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
